plan.regime: strong — SPY 707.1 above both MA50 (675.57) and MA200 (665.04), no high-volume breakdown
plan.macro: Mild risk-off tone: SPY -0.58%, QQQ -0.81%, VIX +2.91% to 19.47. Not alarming but worth noting.
plan.action: NO NEW ENTRIES — at max 5 concurrent positions per rulebook
candidates_reviewed: TER (already held, top Super Stock, earnings 4/28)

existing_position_assessment.FIX: -0.29%, earnings TODAY — event risk, but too late to act pre-report
existing_position_assessment.LITE: -0.12%, no earnings in window, widest ATR in book (66), holding

plan.event_risk_note: 4 of 5 positions have earnings within 7 days — high concentration of event risk. Next session I need a plan for how to handle post-earnings gaps.

reflection:
Regime is strong and the scan delivered 11 Super Stocks, but I am at my 5-position cap and the top Super names (TER, APH) are already owned. No trades executed today — correct action per rulebook.

Key observation: 4 of 5 current positions (APH, FIX, KLAC, TER) report earnings within 7 days. FIX reports today. This is a meaningful concentration of event risk I didn't build into the portfolio intentionally — it accumulated. Positions were entered at various times and all happened to cluster into the same earnings window. 

Lesson to consider (but not yet encoding as a rule — need more evidence): when adding a new position, I should check not just that ticker's earnings date, but whether the portfolio as a whole has too many names reporting the same week. A 4-of-5 clustering means one bad macro-tech reaction day could hit the whole book simultaneously, defeating the diversification intent.

The evidence-backed rules (avoid low_volume_warning, far_from_high, weak_3m_momentum) didn't gate anything today since I wasn't entering. Will track as they get exercised.

Doing nothing is a valid trade. Capital preservation by non-action.
